interactions.summary: Jiang extends the environmental-collapse forecast, then answers the host's dominant-nation question by naming German and Japanese resilience, and distinguishes religious Greater Israel from transnational-capital Pax Judaica.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

speaker_notes.note: Diarization is consistent enough for this packet: SPEAKER_00 carries host questions, intro, outro, or sponsor reads; SPEAKER_01 carries Jiang answers. Sponsor ad segments are not Jiang claims.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts a next-20-years perfect storm of environmental catastrophe that can shatter nation-states into resource-fighting city-states.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction made on 2026-04-15 for roughly the 2040s.

claims.claim: Jiang distinguishes the Greater Israel Project as a religious Zionist territorial and messianic program, while Pax Judaica is transnational capital financing that project into an AI surveillance empire.
claims.temporal_scope: Definition and diagnosis in the 2026-04-15 interview.

signature_moments.moment: The nation-state gives way to city-states fighting over water.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the nation state then gives way to the city state
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the institutional consequence of the catastrophe model.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang says never bet against the Japanese after rehearsing Mongol, Meiji, and postwar resilience.
signature_moments.source_phrase: never, ever bet against the Japanese
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses his civilizational resilience model.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Religious Zionist project to achieve the Promised Land from Egypt to Iraq and beyond, with Jerusalem and the Third Temple as eschatological center.
Transnational-capital project to use Greater Israel as a base for finance, mercenaries, imported labor, energy, data centers, and AI surveillance.

chronology_notes.note: The interview dates Jiang's Greater Israel/Pax Judaica distinction to 2026-04-15 during the Middle East war context.
